etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") denotes an individual or human being. 民(民) (mín, "people") signifies the people or populace. Together, 人民(人民) (rénmín, "the people") represents the collective body of individuals within a community or nation.
etymology.interpretation: 人(人) (rén, "person") signifies an individual human, while 民(民) (mín, "people") refers to the collective populace. The combination of these characters captures the concept of all individuals forming a nation or society. The image of many 人(人) (rén, "person") constituting a single 民(民) (mín, "people") group effectively conveys the idea of a community or population as a whole.

examples.chinese: 人民(人民)是国家的基础(是國家的基礎)。
examples.english: The people are the foundation of the nation.

examples.chinese: 政府应该服务人民(政府應該服務人民)，不是官僚(不是官僚)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèngfǔ yīnggāi fúwù rénmín, bùshì guānliáo.
examples.english: The government should serve the people, not bureaucrats.

examples.chinese: 人民有权利决定自己的未来(人民有權利決定自己的未來)。
examples.pinyin: Rénmín yǒu quánlì juédìng zìjǐ de wèilái.
examples.english: The people have the right to decide their own future.
